rulings.Case Title: PETER M OWENS VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioners' Petition For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 6, 2019 line 1. Petitioners' Petition For Writ Of Mandate DENIED. The Court finds two of the Planning Commission's findings which disapproved Petitioners' application are substantially supported by evidence, i.e. 1) Iris Canada was a tenant (as defined by SF Subd. Code § 1308(j)), who was later displaced from the Property; and 2) that incorrect information was knowingly submitted during the application process. (Breneric Associates v. City of Del Mar (1998) 69 Cal.App.4th 166, 175.) The Court makes no determinations with respect to the Commission's other separate and independent findings. Petitioners have failed to demonstrate that they are entitled to relief on other legal grounds. First, the Court finds that Petitioners' preemption argument is subject to the exhaustion doctrine. (See Coalition for Student Action v. City of Fullerton (1984) 153 Cal.App.3d 1194, 1197.) Petitioners have not shown administrative exhaustion of this issue. Second, Petitioners failed to show facts demonstrating extensive reliance meeting heightened burden to assert estoppel against the City. (Penn-Co v. Board of Supervisors (1984) 158 Cal.App.3d 1072,1081.) Third, the Planning Commission's decision bears rational relationship to a permissible state objective, and therefore, there is no Equal Protection violation. (See Breneric Associates, supra, 69 Cal.App.4th at 187.) = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: SOO MING LAI VS. MARIA NGUYEN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike (Anti-Slapp) Cross-Complainants Fifth Cause Of Action For Intentional Infliction Of Emotional Distress
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 6, 2019 line 7. CROSS DEFENDANT ALLEN LAI, GINA CHU Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike (Anti-Slapp) Cross-Complainants Fifth Cause Of Action For Intentional Infliction Of Emotional Distress DENIED in part and GRANTED in part. The motion is DENIED as to paragraphs 51, 52, 53 and items #1 and #3 in the prayer for relief. There are numerous allegations sufficient to sustain the 5th COA, and the resulting damages requests in the aforementioned paragraphs and prayers for relief. The motion is GRANTED as to the allegations in paragraphs 20, 21, 22 as incorporated into the 5th Cause of Action against Cross Defendants ALLEN, MEW and CHU only. The 30 day notice discussed in the paragraphs 20-22 was in furtherance of protected speech. (CCP §425.16(e).) The Court notes that the litigation privilege would not apply to cross-defendants ALLEN, MEW, and CHU as they were "nonparticipants and nonlitigants to [the] judicial proceedings." (See Wise v. Thrifty Payless, Inc. (2000) 83 Cal.App.4th 1296,1304.) However, Cross-complainant failed to meet its burden in showing that paragraphs 20-22 support a claim for recovery of the 5th Cause of Action.(See Baral V. Schnitt (2016) 1 Cal.5th 376, 488.) More specifically, the Cross-complainant failed to show why an unlawful detainer filed by SOO MING LAI would support the recovery of an IIED claim against ALLEN, MEW, and CHU. Accordingly, paragraphs 20-22 are stricken. Because the motion has been granted in part and denied in part, and the results of the motion "were so insignificant that the party did not achieve any practical benefit from bringing the motion" the Court determines in its discretion that cross-defendants were not the prevailing party for the purposes of attorneys fees. (See Mann v. Quality Old Time Service (2006) 139 Cal.App.4th 328, 345 [disapproved by Baral v. Schnitt (2016) 1 Cal.5th 376 on other grounds only].) The Court also notes that cross-complainant filed two oppositions without permission of the Court and the cross-defendant failed to file a timely reply. = (501/CFH)
